text: Abstract The world of work is undergoing profound change, driven by a complex array of factors including political instability, increasing income inequality, job insecurity and poor working conditions. In response, international organizations are beginning to make policy recommendations aimed at improving the health and wellbeing of workers. This article offers a first-time account of the influence of the International Labour Organization’s (ILO) Decent Work Agenda on the work policy agendas of the World Health Organization (WHO) and the World Bank.
